name: Plandex
description: Open-source, plan-first terminal agent with a cumulative-diff review sandbox and up to 2M-token context for large multi-file tasks.

developer.comment: Built by Plandex Inc. (plandex-ai on GitHub), founded 2024.

first-release.comment: First public launch April 2024; v1.0.0 May 2024; v2 (major rewrite) released April 16, 2025.

status.comment: Actively maintained open-source project (v2.x through 2025). Hosted Plandex Cloud is winding down (Oct 2025) in favor of self-hosted/local mode, but the software itself remains active.

specialization.comment: Broad coding agent, positioned for large multi-file/large-project tasks but general-purpose.

interface.comment: Terminal-only CLI with a REPL; no IDE or editor integration.

host-ides.comment: Runs in the terminal (Linux, macOS, WSL on Windows).

self-contained-vs-plugin.comment: Standalone CLI tool with a client/server architecture; server can be self-hosted.

default-model-provider.comment: Default model packs combine the best models from Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google; no single default provider.

model-choice.comment: Curated model packs and custom model packs let users mix models across Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, OpenRouter, and others.

bring-your-own-key.comment: BYOK via direct provider accounts or OpenRouter; self-hosted/local mode is fully BYOK.

local-model-support.comment: Built-in Ollama support (ollama-oss and ollama-daily model packs); requires self-hosting. Also accepts OpenAI-compatible custom providers.

multi-file-editing.comment: Core capability: plans and applies coordinated changes across dozens of files in one task. (A third-party tracker lists this as No, but the official site explicitly markets multi-file/large-task editing.)

autonomous-task-execution.comment: Full autonomy mode loads context, plans, implements, executes commands, and auto-debugs end-to-end; autonomy is configurable from none to full.

terminal-command-execution.comment: Runs builds, linters, tests, deploys, and scripts and automatically debugs failures.

mcp-support.comment: MCP not shipped as of check (2026-06-05); tracked as an open feature request (issue #241). Checked 2026-06.

subagents-parallel-agents.comment: Single linear planning/execution loop; no documented sub-agent or parallel-agent orchestration.

codebase-indexing.comment: Builds tree-sitter project maps (structural, not a semantic embedding index) and the LLM selects context from them; this is not embeddings-based semantic indexing.

planning-mode.comment: Plan-first design: a project-aware chat/planning mode flesh-out before implementation; plans are a first-class, version-controlled concept.

multimodal-input.comment: Conflicting signals: Plandex docs list 'images' among loadable context types via the load command, but an independent tracker (Terminal Trove, Apr 2026) reports image/multimodal input as No. Left null pending clearer confirmation. Checked 2026-06.

browser-computer-use.comment: With Chrome installed, Plandex can launch a browser, read console logs/errors, and automatically debug browser applications as part of its loop.

autonomous-subagent-spawning.comment: No subagent/parallel-agent capability exists (subagents-parallel-agents is false): Plandex runs a single linear plan-then-execute loop, so there is no model-driven delegation. Per RESEARCH.md, false when the underlying capability is absent. Checked 2026-06.

operating-mode.comment: Spans interactive chat/planning, step-by-step supervised execution, and full autonomous task completion (configurable autonomy levels). No inline completion; no detached async/background runner.

inline-completion.comment: Terminal CLI agent; no tab-style as-you-type code completion.

background-async-execution.comment: Runs interactively in the terminal session; no detached cloud/background task dispatch where the user walks away. (Hosted cloud is winding down.)

persistent-memory.comment: Plans persist across sessions with version-controlled history and branches; conversation/context for a plan is retained and can be resumed.

checkpoints-rollback.comment: Cumulative diff-review sandbox keeps changes separate until applied; version-controlled plan updates allow rewind/rollback to prior points.

git-integration.comment: Native git awareness with automatic commit-message generation; changes can be applied and committed from the agent.

pr-creation.comment: No documented pull-request creation; git integration stops at applying/committing changes locally.

custom-rules-instructions.comment: Supports user 'notes' loaded into a plan's context as sticky instructions (e.g. 'add logging to all generated code'), plus arbitrary files/dirs loaded as context.

hooks-extensibility.comment: No programmable hooks or plugin API documented.

permission-model.comment: Configurable autonomy from step-by-step approval to full auto; the diff sandbox gates application of changes for review. Sandbox isolates pending changes rather than sandboxing command execution.

self-host-on-prem.comment: Self-hostable server (Docker / Railway) and a fully local mode; self-hosting is now the recommended path as Plandex Cloud winds down.

privacy-mode.comment: Self-hosted/local mode keeps code on the user's own infrastructure; with local Ollama models no code leaves the machine. Training-data exposure depends on the BYOK provider chosen, not Plandex.

enterprise-controls.comment: No documented SSO/RBAC/audit-log/VPC governance features; positioned as developer/self-hosted OSS rather than an enterprise product.

pricing-model.comment: The software is free/open-source; users pay only their own LLM API costs (BYOK). Hosted cloud is winding down; no subscription plan for the tool itself.

free-tier.comment: Free open-source software; self-hosting has no license fee (only infra + LLM API costs).

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: No paid plan for the tool; it is free/BYOK. Users incur LLM API costs (~$10-$100/mo for heavy use) but that is provider billing, not a Plandex plan.

maturity.comment: Usable and actively maintained (v2 major rewrite Apr 2025), but evolving and described by reviewers as research-grade OSS without commercial backing since cloud wound down.

community-size.comment: ~15K GitHub stars; reviewers describe a smaller, single-vendor ecosystem relative to Aider/Cursor. Checked 2026-06.

extension-plugin-ecosystem.comment: No marketplace or meaningful third-party plugin ecosystem; no MCP support to anchor one.

project-memory-files.comment: No auto-loaded repo-resident instruction-file convention (no AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md auto-load). Instructions are added via in-plan 'notes' or by loading files into context explicitly.

memory-scopes.comment: Context/notes are scoped to a plan within a project; no documented enterprise/user-global/per-folder rule scopes.

scoped-rules-files.comment: No conditional/path-based or file-type-based rule-file activation mechanism; context is loaded explicitly per plan.

auto-memory.comment: No automatic cross-session memory the agent writes on its own; context is user-managed (load/notes).

skill-support.comment: No reusable user-authored skill/recipe mechanism; the REPL exposes only built-in CLI commands. No hooks/extensions either.

skill-invocation.comment: Not applicable; Plandex has no skill mechanism (skill-support is false).

skill-distribution.comment: No skills supported, so no distribution mechanism.

sandboxing-technology.comment: The 'cumulative diff review sandbox' only stages pending file changes (a version-controlled buffer reviewed before applying); it is not execution isolation. Commands run directly with the user's privileges, and the docs recommend a clean git state / an isolated branch before using auto-exec rather than relying on any kernel/container sandbox. Checked 2026-06.

permission-granularity.comment: Gating is a single global autonomy level (none/basic/plus/semi/full) plus a diff-review approval step; no per-tool, per-command, command-prefix, or per-path allowlist. Higher autonomy auto-loads context (reads) while still staging writes, but there is no user-configurable read/write rule, so it stays all-or-nothing. Checked 2026-06.

rule-precedence.comment: No declarative allow/deny permission rule engine; permissioning is a preset autonomy level, so there is no precedence model to express. Checked 2026-06.

permission-configurability.comment: User picks among predefined autonomy presets (none, basic, plus, semi, full) via set-auto, settable per-plan and as a persisted default for new plans. No user-authored allow/deny rules and no programmable permission hooks/callbacks. Checked 2026-06.

sensitive-file-protection.comment: No documented out-of-the-box deny patterns for secrets (.env, key files); files enter context only when explicitly loaded, but there is no shipped built-in refusal/gate for sensitive files. Checked 2026-06.

persistent-allowlist.comment: No per-action 'always allow this command' allowlist that is remembered. Autonomy/config presets do persist (per-plan and as a default for new plans), but that sets an overall posture rather than recording specific approved actions to skip re-prompting. Checked 2026-06.

unsupervised-mode.comment: Full autonomy mode (set-auto full / --auto-exec) auto-applies changes, executes commands, and debugs end-to-end with no approval prompts; docs warn it 'can make many changes quickly without any prompting or review' and run potentially destructive commands. Checked 2026-06.

network-access-control.comment: No documented outbound-network gating; executed commands run with the user's normal, unrestricted network access (no egress block, domain allowlist, or proxy). Checked 2026-06.

default-permission-posture.comment: Out of the box (autonomy below full), changes are staged in the diff sandbox for review and the user is prompted to approve execution/debugging steps; only the opt-in full mode auto-runs everything. Exact default preset name not confirmed from docs (page unreachable at check), but non-full levels all prompt before applying/running. Checked 2026-06.

auto-accept-mode.comment: Auto-apply is a distinct, independently-toggleable middle rung between per-action prompting and full bypass. `set-config auto-apply true` (or `\set-config` in the REPL, or a per-command `--apply`/`-a` flag) auto-applies edits from the diff sandbox while auto-exec stays off by default, so commands still prompt. This is lighter than full-auto/unsupervised mode (`set-auto full`, which additionally auto-execs and auto-debugs). Checked 2026-07.
